skill.name: obsidian-markdown
skill.description: Create Obsidian-compatible Markdown notes with wikilinks, embeds, callouts, properties, tables, math, diagrams, and vault-friendly references.
skill.summary: Create and revise Markdown that follows Obsidian syntax and note-linking conventions.
skill.author: ZhanlinCui

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command execution, weak crypto, reconnaissance, and URL patterns. Review found the command, crypto, and reconnaissance alerts are Markdown documentation false positives. The remaining URLs are examples or documentation references, with no automated network access or exfiltration intent.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static command alerts are Markdown fences and inline code examples. They are documentation syntax, not Ruby or shell execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is inside Markdown code fences or inline examples. No executable script, interpreter call, or command invocation is present.

low_findings.title: Reference URLs Are Documentation Links
low_findings.description: Hardcoded URLs point to public Markdown, Obsidian, LaTeX, or example documentation. The skill does not fetch or transmit data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are rendered as links or examples. I found no code that opens network connections or sends user content externally.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto Alerts Are Word Matches
low_findings.description: The high blocker alerts match ordinary Markdown text such as description, type labels, nesting guidance, and frontend design wording.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines do not contain cryptographic functions or algorithms. They are prose, table headers, or note content examples.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Alerts Are Markdown Content
low_findings.description: System reconnaissance alerts match normal documentation examples, block IDs, Mermaid graph syntax, and note text. No host inspection is requested.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are example note syntax and diagram syntax. I found no commands for host, network, user, or environment enumeration.

content.value_statement: Obsidian notes can break when links, embeds, callouts, and properties use the wrong syntax. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code produce vault-ready Markdown.
actual_capabilities: Writes Obsidian wikilinks with aliases, headings, and block references.
Formats callouts, nested callouts, and foldable callout sections.
Creates YAML properties for tags, aliases, dates, status, and custom fields.
Adds embeds for notes, images, audio, PDFs, lists, and search results.
Uses Obsidian-compatible tables, footnotes, comments, math, and Mermaid diagrams.
Escapes Markdown characters that would otherwise change note rendering.
limitations: It does not inspect an Obsidian vault unless files are provided in the workspace.
It cannot verify that linked note names already exist without vault context.
It does not install Obsidian plugins or modify application settings.
Advanced rendering can vary when community themes or plugins change syntax behavior.

use_cases.title: Build linked research notes
use_cases.description: Create literature notes with wikilinks, aliases, headings, footnotes, tags, and source references.

use_cases.title: Document projects in a vault
use_cases.description: Turn project information into structured notes with properties, task lists, callouts, and related document links.

use_cases.title: Standardize team knowledge bases
use_cases.description: Rewrite inconsistent Markdown into Obsidian-friendly pages with predictable sections, embeds, tables, and metadata.

prompt_templates.title: Create a basic note
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an Obsidian note about [topic]. Include a title, short summary, tags, and three wikilinks to related notes.

prompt_templates.title: Convert text into vault format
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this text into Obsidian Markdown. Add properties, callouts for important points, and wikilinks for key concepts: [paste text].

prompt_templates.title: Design a project note template
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an Obsidian project note template with YAML properties, status callouts, task lists, decision logs, and related document links.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor a complex vault page
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Obsidian note and improve its structure, links, embeds, block references, callouts, tables, and properties: [paste note].

output_examples.input: Create a project planning note for a migration project.
output_examples.output: A structured Obsidian note with properties, status callouts, task lists, related links, and a decisions section.

output_examples.input: Convert meeting notes into Obsidian format.
output_examples.output: A cleaned meeting note with attendees, action items, nested tasks, highlights, and links to project pages.

output_examples.input: Improve a research note about graph databases.
output_examples.output: A linked research note with aliases, tags, citations, definition callouts, footnotes, and related concept links.

best_practices: Provide the target vault naming style before asking for wikilinks.
Ask for properties and tags that match your existing note templates.
Review generated links when the model cannot see the full vault.
anti_patterns: Do not ask it to confirm that linked files exist without providing vault context.
Do not mix several incompatible note templates in one request.
Do not rely on external embeds when offline vault use is required.

faq.question: Does this skill create valid Obsidian wikilinks?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers note links, aliases, heading links, same-note links, and block references.

faq.question: Can it add YAML properties?
faq.answer: Yes. It can create properties for tags, aliases, dates, status, ratings, and custom fields.

faq.question: Does it require Obsidian to be installed?
faq.answer: No. It writes Markdown syntax. Obsidian is only needed to render and manage the vault.

faq.question: Can it check whether a note exists?
faq.answer: Only when the relevant vault files are available in the workspace or provided by the user.

faq.question: Does it support callouts and embeds?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents basic, foldable, nested callouts, and embeds for notes, media, PDFs, and search results.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found documentation-only examples and links, with no executable behavior or exfiltration pattern.
